items.prompt: What does Make primarily read to decide what to run?
choices: A Makefile with targets, deps, and recipes
items.explain: Make executes rules from a Makefile based on targets and dependencies.

choices: So Make ignores the target forever
So Make always runs the recipe even if a file named clean exists
items.explain: .PHONY marks names that are actions, not real output files.

items.prompt: Running make with no arguments usually builds the default (often first or all) target.
items.explain: Bare make runs the default goal—commonly all or the first target listed.

items.prompt: Recipe lines under a Makefile rule must be indented with…
items.explain: Make requires a tab before each recipe command (spaces alone fail).
